message:
fix(packs/remote/scp): close path-traversal bypass via /tmp/../

The scp pack's safe patterns for /tmp, /var/tmp, and ~ accepted any
path starting with that prefix, which let `..` directory traversal
escape the sandbox. For example:

  scp secret user@host:/tmp/../etc/passwd

...would match `scp-to-tmp` (safe) and short-circuit before any
destructive pattern could see that the path actually resolves to
`/etc/passwd` on the remote host.

Added `(?!\S*\.\./)` negative lookahead to each of `scp-to-tmp`,
`scp-to-var-tmp`, and `scp-to-home`. `..foo` filenames still work;
only `../` directory traversal is blocked.

Regression test added. Core filesystem `rm` safe patterns already
have equivalent `..` protection; this brings scp to parity.
